title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A noble family takes stewardship of a desert planet that is the only source of a substance prized across the galactic empire. A young heir navigates prophecy, betrayal and ecological survival as warring houses fight for control of the spice.

title: Dune Messiah
author: Frank Herbert
description: The sequel follows the emperor who rose from the desert as he confronts the costs of power and the religion built around him. A conspiracy of priests and navigators plots against the throne on the desert world.

title: The Left Hand of Darkness
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: An envoy arrives on a frozen planet whose inhabitants have no fixed sex, and must navigate alien politics and a long journey across the ice to forge an alliance between worlds.

title: A Wizard of Earthsea
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A gifted boy on an archipelago is sent to a school for wizards, unleashes a shadow through pride, and must journey across the islands to confront the darkness he set loose.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A home-loving hobbit is swept into an adventure with dwarves and a wizard to reclaim a treasure guarded by a dragon, growing braver across a long journey there and back again.

title: The Lord of the Rings
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A hobbit inherits a ring of terrible power and joins a fellowship on an epic quest across a vast world to destroy it before a dark lord can conquer everything.

title: Mistborn
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: In a world of ash and oppression a street thief discovers she can fuel magic by burning metals, and joins a daring crew plotting to topple an immortal tyrant.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: A gifted orphan recounts his rise from poverty to a legendary arcane university, chasing the truth about the creatures that murdered his family.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A celebrated painter shoots her husband then never speaks again. A psychotherapist becomes obsessed with unlocking her silence, and the truth arrives as a twist few readers see coming.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: When a woman vanishes on her wedding anniversary her husband becomes the prime suspect, but alternating accounts reveal a marriage built on lies and a chilling, twisting deception.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: A disgraced journalist and a brilliant antisocial hacker investigate a wealthy family's decades-old disappearance, uncovering buried violence in a cold Scandinavian setting.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
description: Ten strangers are lured to a remote island where they are killed one by one according to an old rhyme, and the survivors realize the murderer is among them.

title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
author: Agatha Christie
description: A retired detective investigates a wealthy man's murder in an English village, in a classic whodunit famous for one of the genre's most audacious twists.

title: Murder on the Orient Express
author: Agatha Christie
description: A detective trapped by snow aboard a stalled luxury train must unmask which passenger murdered an American tycoon before the line is cleared.

title: The Tokyo Zodiac Murders
author: Soji Shimada
description: An astrologer and an illustrator reinvestigate a decades-old series of grisly killings in Japan built on an elaborate astrological puzzle that has baffled detectives.

title: Malice
author: Keigo Higashino
description: A bestselling novelist is found murdered in his locked study in Tokyo, and a methodical detective must untangle a confession that hides a darker motive.

title: The Devotion of Suspect X
author: Keigo Higashino
description: A quiet mathematics teacher in Japan constructs a perfect alibi to protect the neighbor he loves after she kills her abusive ex, but a rival physicist sees through it.

title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
author: TJ Klune
description: A by-the-book caseworker is sent to assess an orphanage of magical children on a sunlit island and finds unexpected warmth, love and a found family worth fighting for.

title: Legends & Lattes
author: Travis Baldree
description: A battle-weary orc retires from adventuring to open a coffee shop in a fantasy city, and builds a gentle, low-stakes life and a found family among quirky regulars.

title: A Man Called Ove
author: Fredrik Backman
description: A grumpy widower whose rigid routines mask deep grief is slowly pulled back into life by boisterous new neighbors in a heartwarming, feel-good story of community.

title: The House in the Night
author: Susan Marie
description: In a quiet seaside town a retired teacher reconnects with estranged family over a single summer, a gentle warm-hearted story of second chances and belonging.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
description: Two Irish teenagers from different classes circle each other through school and university in a quiet, precise study of intimacy, miscommunication and love.

title: A Little Life
author: Hanya Yanagihara
description: Four college friends move to New York, but the novel centers on one man's hidden history of abuse in a devastating, deeply character-driven and sorrowful epic.

title: Beloved
author: Toni Morrison
description: A formerly enslaved woman in post-Civil-War Ohio is haunted by the ghost of her dead daughter, a towering, sorrowful meditation on memory and freedom.

title: Klara and the Sun
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: An artificial friend observes the human world with tender attention as she is chosen to care for an ailing child, a quiet, sorrowful meditation on love and what it means to be human.

title: Never Let Me Go
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: Students at a strange English boarding school slowly learn the heartbreaking purpose of their sheltered lives, a quiet and devastating dystopia about love and mortality.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
description: An astronaut stranded alone on Mars must use ingenuity, botany and relentless humor to survive and signal Earth in a propulsive, upbeat tale of problem-solving.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone amnesiac astronaut wakes far from Earth on a desperate mission to save humanity, and an unexpected alien friendship powers a witty, upbeat survival adventure.

title: Snow Crash
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A pizza-delivering hacker and a teenage skateboard courier race through a fractured near-future America and a virtual metaverse to stop a mind-shattering digital plague.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: A washed-up console cowboy is hired for one last hack through cyberspace, pulled into a noir conspiracy involving rogue artificial intelligences in a neon dystopia.

title: Roadside Picnic
author: Arkady and Boris Strugatsky
description: In zones left behind by an inexplicable alien visit, scavengers risk death to retrieve strange artifacts, an eerie and obscure meditation on the unknowable.

title: Solaris
author: Stanislaw Lem
description: Scientists orbiting a sentient ocean planet are confronted by manifestations of their own memories, an eerie and cerebral study of the limits of human understanding.

title: Convenience Store Woman
author: Sayaka Murata
description: A woman in Tokyo who has worked at a convenience store for eighteen years resists society's expectations in a sharp, offbeat and quietly subversive short novel.

title: Before the Coffee Gets Cold
author: Toshikazu Kawaguchi
description: In a tiny Tokyo cafe a particular seat lets customers travel back in time for as long as a coffee stays warm, a gentle, cozy and heartwarming set of linked stories.

title: Strange Weather in Tokyo
author: Hiromi Kawakami
description: A lonely woman in Tokyo strikes up a tender, unhurried romance with her former teacher over food and sake, a quiet and gently melancholic love story.

title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: A detective and his companion investigate a spectral hound said to haunt a moorland family, untangling a very human plot behind the gothic legend.

title: Children of Time
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
description: A terraforming experiment gone wrong uplifts a species of spiders over millennia while the last humans search for a new home, an epic of evolution and first contact.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Cixin Liu
description: A secret military project makes contact with an alien civilization facing the chaos of three suns, setting humanity on a collision course in a vast, cerebral epic.

title: Piranesi
author: Susanna Clarke
description: A gentle man lives alone in an endless labyrinth of statues and tides, keeping a journal, until clues suggest his world is not what it seems, an eerie and beautiful puzzle.

title: The Ocean at the End of the Lane
author: Neil Gaiman
description: A man returns home and remembers a strange childhood encounter with an otherworldly family at the farm down the lane, a short, eerie and wistful modern fairy tale.

title: Hyperion
author: Dan Simmons
description: Seven pilgrims travel to a distant world and its deadly time-warping tombs, each telling a story that builds an epic, mournful mosaic of a galaxy on the brink of war.

title: A Fire Upon the Deep
author: Vernor Vinge
description: A reawakened ancient power threatens the galaxy while refugees flee to a remote world of pack-mind aliens, in a sweeping space opera of vast scope and strange biology.

title: Ancillary Justice
author: Ann Leckie
description: A starship's artificial intelligence, now trapped in a single human body, hunts across an interstellar empire for revenge, questioning identity, gender and loyalty.

title: The Dispossessed
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A brilliant physicist travels from his austere anarchist moon to the wealthy world below, in a thoughtful, ideas-driven study of two opposed societies.

title: Annihilation
author: Jeff VanderMeer
description: A biologist joins an expedition into a quarantined zone where nature has gone strange and inexplicable, an eerie, dreamlike descent into the unknowable.

title: The City and the City
author: China Mieville
description: A detective investigates a murder spanning two cities that occupy the same space yet must be unseen by each other, a noir mystery wrapped in an eerie, surreal premise.

title: Jonathan Strange and Mr Norrell
author: Susanna Clarke
description: Two very different magicians revive English magic during the Napoleonic wars, in a witty, footnoted, beautifully written epic of rivalry and faerie.

title: Uprooted
author: Naomi Novik
description: A village girl is taken by a cold wizard to help fight a corrupted, malevolent forest, discovering her own wild magic in a lush standalone fairy tale.

title: The Fifth Season
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: On a world wracked by world-ending catastrophes, people who can command the earth are feared and enslaved, in a furious, inventive epic told in braided timelines.

title: The Priory of the Orange Tree
author: Samantha Shannon
description: Across rival realms a queen without an heir, a dragon-riding warrior and a hidden mage race to stop an ancient wyrm from waking, in a sprawling standalone epic.

title: The Blade Itself
author: Joe Abercrombie
description: A crippled torturer, a vain swordsman and a barbarian are pulled into a wizard's schemes in a gritty, cynical and darkly funny take on epic fantasy.

title: Big Little Lies
author: Liane Moriarty
description: Three mothers in a seaside town are drawn together by secrets and lies that build toward a death at a school fundraiser, a sharp domestic suspense with a final twist.

title: The Woman in the Window
author: A.J. Finn
description: An agoraphobic woman who watches her neighbors believes she has witnessed a crime, but her drinking and unreliable memory make the truth a slippery, twisting puzzle.

title: Sharp Objects
author: Gillian Flynn
description: A troubled reporter returns to her hometown to cover the murders of two girls and confronts her own toxic family, a dark, disturbing psychological thriller with a twist.

title: The Thursday Murder Club
author: Richard Osman
description: Four retirees in a peaceful village meet weekly to investigate cold cases until a real killing lands on their doorstep, a warm, witty and cozy whodunit.

title: The Maid
author: Nita Prose
description: A socially awkward hotel maid becomes the prime suspect when she discovers a guest dead in his suite, a charming, cozy and quirky locked-room mystery.

title: Pachinko
author: Min Jin Lee
description: A sweeping multigenerational saga follows a Korean family enduring poverty, war and prejudice across decades in Japan, a rich and sorrowful historical epic.

title: The Remains of the Day
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: An aging English butler reflects on a life of dignified service and the love he never allowed himself, a quiet, restrained and deeply moving meditation on regret.

title: Stoner
author: John Williams
description: The unremarkable life of a Midwestern university professor unfolds with quiet grace and accumulating sorrow, a plain, beautiful and profoundly moving novel.

title: A Gentleman in Moscow
author: Amor Towles
description: A count sentenced to permanent house arrest in a grand Moscow hotel builds an unexpectedly rich and warm life across decades of upheaval, an elegant and charming tale.

title: Kafka on the Shore
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A runaway teenager and an old man who talks to cats move through an eerie, dreamlike Japan where reality bends, a surreal and hypnotic modern fable.

title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
author: Haruki Murakami
description: An ordinary Tokyo man searching for his wife's missing cat is drawn into a strange, dreamlike labyrinth of war memories and hidden wells, a surreal and haunting epic.

title: Norwegian Wood
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A man recalls his student years in Tokyo and the two women who shaped a season of love, loss and grief, a tender, nostalgic and melancholy coming-of-age.

title: The Long Way to a Small Angry Planet
author: Becky Chambers
description: The diverse crew of an aging tunneling ship take a long job across the galaxy, in a warm, low-stakes and gentle space opera about found family and belonging.
